title: sarcostemma viminale subsp. australe
intro: Sarcostemma viminale subsp. australe is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is commonly known as the 'thorny calf weed' or 'thorny caltrop'. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a diuretic and to reduce blood pressure. The herbal preparations of Sarcostemma viminale subsp. australe include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or topically to harness its medicinal properties.
